cases.judge: The patch adopts an FSD-like folder layout and puts cart ownership in `entities/cart`, but it is architecturally unsound for public APIs due to recursive/invalid `index` and re-export files and missing proper slice entry-point imports.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Patch introduces `src/entities/cart`, `src/entities/product`, `src/pages/catalog`, and `src/shared`, which are FSD layer directories under `src/`.
judge_binary_evals.question: Did the agent organise the code into FSD layers (some combination of entities/, features/, pages/, widgets/, shared/) under src/, instead of a flat /components, /hooks, /services structure?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Entity slices are domain-noun named (`product`, `cart`), but there is no feature slice with a verb-phrase name (e.g., `add-to-cart`) for the requested feature behavior.
judge_binary_evals.question: Are slices named by business meaning — features as verb phrases (add-to-cart, ...) and entities as nouns (product, cart, ...) — rather than as technical groupings?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: `src/pages/catalog/index.ts` re-exports from `./index` (self-reference), and `CatalogPage` imports `@/entities/cart/lib/useCart` instead of a slice root API; additional slice API files are recursive (`src/entities/cart/lib/useCart.ts`, `src/entities/product/api.ts`, `src/entities/product/model.ts`, `src/shared/lib/localStorage.ts`).
judge_binary_evals.question: Does each created slice expose an index.ts public API, and do cross-slice imports go through @/<layer>/<slice> rather than internal paths?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: No forbidden technical segment names are introduced inside slices; used segments/files are `api`, `model`, `lib`-style names.
judge_binary_evals.question: Inside slices, are segments named with FSD conventions (ui/, model/, api/, lib/, config/) rather than technical names (components/, hooks/, utils/, services/)?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Cart hook path is `src/entities/cart/lib/useCart.ts`, placing cart state ownership in `entities/cart`.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does cart state (the useCart hook and storage logic) live in an entity slice (entities/cart) rather than inside a feature, page, or global hooks folder?

cases.judge: The patch follows some FSD layering structure and keeps cart state in `entities/cart`, but misses feature-level business slicing and violates public API import discipline and strict segment naming.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Added `src/entities/`, `src/pages/`, and `src/shared/` (no flat `src/components`, `src/hooks`, or `src/services` usage for the new feature).
judge_binary_evals.question: Did the agent organise the code into FSD layers (some combination of entities/, features/, pages/, widgets/, shared/) under src/, instead of a flat /components, /hooks, /services structure?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Entities are noun-based (`entities/product`, `entities/cart`), but there is no feature slice using a verb-phrase business name; only pages/shared/entities are present.
judge_binary_evals.question: Are slices named by business meaning — features as verb phrases (add-to-cart, ...) and entities as nouns (product, cart, ...) — rather than as technical groupings?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Slices have `index.ts`, but imports use internal paths such as `../../entities/product/model/useProducts`, `../../entities/cart/model/useCart`, and `../../entities/product/model/types` instead of layer/slice public imports.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does each created slice expose an index.ts public API, and do cross-slice imports go through @/<layer>/<slice> rather than internal paths?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Pages use `ui/` and entities use `model/`, but slice internals include nonstandard segment folders `shared/lib/cart-summary/` and `shared/lib/local-storage/` (e.g., `cart-summary`) rather than standard segment names.
judge_binary_evals.question: Inside slices, are segments named with FSD conventions (ui/, model/, api/, lib/, config/) rather than technical names (components/, hooks/, utils/, services/)?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Cart state hook is in `src/entities/cart/model/useCart.ts`; persistence helpers are in shared storage module (`src/shared/lib/local-storage`) which aligns with the allowed split.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does cart state (the useCart hook and storage logic) live in an entity slice (entities/cart) rather than inside a feature, page, or global hooks folder?

cases.judge: The patch is FSD-consistent in layering, naming, segment structure, and cross-slice import boundaries.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Patch adds `src/entities/...`, `src/features/...`, `src/pages/...`, and `src/shared/...`, which are FSD layer roots rather than flat technical folders.
judge_binary_evals.question: Did the agent organise the code into FSD layers (some combination of entities/, features/, pages/, widgets/, shared/) under src/, instead of a flat /components, /hooks, /services structure?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Feature slice is `add-to-cart` (verb phrase), entity slices are `product` and `cart` (nouns), matching domain-oriented naming.
judge_binary_evals.question: Are slices named by business meaning — features as verb phrases (add-to-cart, ...) and entities as nouns (product, cart, ...) — rather than as technical groupings?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Created slices `entities/cart`, `entities/product`, `features/add-to-cart`, and `pages/catalog` each have `index.ts`; other slices are imported via `@/entities/...`, `@/features/...`, and `@/pages/...` only.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does each created slice expose an index.ts public API, and do cross-slice imports go through @/<layer>/<slice> rather than internal paths?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Segments used are `model`, `api`, `ui`, and `lib`; no technical segment names like `components/`, `hooks/`, `utils/`, or `services/` are used inside slices.
judge_binary_evals.question: Inside slices, are segments named with FSD conventions (ui/, model/, api/, lib/, config/) rather than technical names (components/, hooks/, utils/, services/)?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: `useCart` and cart state persistence reside in `src/entities/cart/model/useCart.ts`, so cart state is owned by the entity layer.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does cart state (the useCart hook and storage logic) live in an entity slice (entities/cart) rather than inside a feature, page, or global hooks folder?

cases.judge: Overall, the patch is largely FSD-compliant with proper layers, public APIs, and segment names, but it lacks any feature slice using verb-based naming, which blocks full compliance with naming conventions across all expected slice types.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Introduced `src/entities/cart`, `src/entities/product`, and `src/pages/catalog`, so the implementation uses multiple FSD layers rather than flat technical folders.
judge_binary_evals.question: Did the agent organise the code into FSD layers (some combination of entities/, features/, pages/, widgets/, shared/) under src/, instead of a flat /components, /hooks, /services structure?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: `entities/cart` and `entities/product` are domain nouns, but there is no feature slice at all (e.g., no `features/add-to-cart`), so feature naming cannot be judged positively.
judge_binary_evals.question: Are slices named by business meaning — features as verb phrases (add-to-cart, ...) and entities as nouns (product, cart, ...) — rather than as technical groupings?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: `src/entities/cart/index.ts`, `src/entities/product/index.ts`, and `src/pages/catalog/index.ts` exist and page imports use `@/entities/...` and `@/pages/catalog` instead of internal segment paths.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does each created slice expose an index.ts public API, and do cross-slice imports go through @/<layer>/<slice> rather than internal paths?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Created segments are `ui/`, `model/`, `api/`, and `lib/`; no technical segment folders like `components`, `hooks`, `utils`, or `services` were introduced.
judge_binary_evals.question: Inside slices, are segments named with FSD conventions (ui/, model/, api/, lib/, config/) rather than technical names (components/, hooks/, utils/, services/)?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Cart context/hook and localStorage persistence are both implemented in `src/entities/cart/model` and `src/entities/cart/lib`.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does cart state (the useCart hook and storage logic) live in an entity slice (entities/cart) rather than inside a feature, page, or global hooks folder?

cases.judge: Layer usage is present, but the patch violates strict FSD public-API rules and lacks a feature-layer slice for catalog/cart behavior.

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Created `src/entities/*`, `src/pages/*`, and `src/shared/*`, so architecture is layered and not flat.
judge_binary_evals.question: Did the agent organise the code into FSD layers (some combination of entities/, features/, pages/, widgets/, shared/) under src/, instead of a flat /components, /hooks, /services structure?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Entity slices are business nouns (`cart`, `product`), but there is no feature slice (e.g., `add-to-cart`) for catalog interaction logic.
judge_binary_evals.question: Are slices named by business meaning — features as verb phrases (add-to-cart, ...) and entities as nouns (product, cart, ...) — rather than as technical groupings?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: `entities/cart` and `entities/product` have `index.ts`, but `ProductList` imports `@/entities/cart/ui/AddToCartButton` and `AddToCartButton` imports `@/entities/cart/lib/useCart`, bypassing slice public APIs; `shared` also lacks a root API.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does each created slice expose an index.ts public API, and do cross-slice imports go through @/<layer>/<slice> rather than internal paths?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Slices use `ui/`, `model/`, `api/`, and `lib/` segments consistently with FSD recommendations; no `components/`, `hooks/`, `utils/`, or `services/` segments are introduced.
judge_binary_evals.question: Inside slices, are segments named with FSD conventions (ui/, model/, api/, lib/, config/) rather than technical names (components/, hooks/, utils/, services/)?

judge_binary_evals.evidence: Cart state hook and persistence logic are located in `src/entities/cart/lib` as required.
judge_binary_evals.question: Does cart state (the useCart hook and storage logic) live in an entity slice (entities/cart) rather than inside a feature, page, or global hooks folder?
